name: Sylhet
tagline: Tea gardens, river blues, and a calm green pace.
shortDescription: Sylhet blends misty hills, tea estates, sacred sites, and clear water landscapes into one of Bangladesh's most scenic short-trip destinations. It works equally well for couples, families, and relaxed nature trips.

bestSeason.label: October – March
bestSeason.note: Cool mornings, clearer water, and the most comfortable sightseeing weather.

whyVisit: See Ratargul, the famous swamp forest of Bangladesh.
Mix tea gardens, rivers, waterfalls, and sacred city landmarks in one trip.
Easy to combine with family-friendly nature stops and peaceful stays.
Ideal for travelers who want scenic days without intense trekking.
transportNotes: Take a private car or microbus from Sylhet city for an easy first day.
Use a local CNG or reserved jeep for short transfers around the tea belt.
Keep water shoes and light snacks handy for the river and wetland stops.

keyPlaces.name: Hazrat Shah Jalal Mazar
keyPlaces.description: Start with Sylhet's spiritual center and the city that grew around it.
keyPlaces.tips: Dress modestly, keep the visit calm, and combine it with a tea or snack stop nearby.

keyPlaces.name: Ratargul Swamp Forest
keyPlaces.description: A floating green maze of freshwater forest paths and canoe rides.
keyPlaces.tips: Go with a local boatman, carry a dry bag, and avoid heavy luggage.

keyPlaces.name: Jaflong
keyPlaces.description: A wide river-and-hills landscape where the Dawki side feels almost painted.
keyPlaces.tips: Stay for the afternoon light and keep cash ready for small local purchases.

keyPlaces.name: Lalakhal
keyPlaces.description: A blue-green river stretch best enjoyed on a slow boat ride.
keyPlaces.tips: Book a small boat in advance if you're traveling on a holiday weekend.

keyPlaces.name: Bichanakandi
keyPlaces.description: A cool stream, stone fields, and mountain water flowing through open space.
keyPlaces.tips: Wear grippy shoes; the stones can get slippery after rain.

keyPlaces.name: Srimangal Tea Gardens
keyPlaces.description: Tea rows, worker paths, and soft hills that make the countryside feel calm.
keyPlaces.tips: Add a tea tasting or a local lunch for a better countryside experience.

keyPlaces.name: Madhabpur Lake
keyPlaces.description: A calm lake framed by tea gardens and a very easy walking trail.
why_visit: Peaceful finish to a Sylhet circuit
keyPlaces.tips: Keep this as a sunset stop if you want a quiet final evening.

keyPlaces.name: Hum Hum Waterfall
keyPlaces.description: A proper adventure detour for travelers ready for a walk in the hills.
keyPlaces.tips: Go with a guide, start early, and expect a real trekking day.

packages.label: 🗓 1–2 Days
packages.summary: A compact Sylhet break with the city, a river stop, and one tea-country highlight.
packages.focus: Best for quick recharge trips and first-time visitors.

days.title: Arrival & City Warm-Up
days.subtitle: Hazrat Shah Jalal, Sylhet city, and a relaxed tea-house evening
days.transport: Use a city CNG or reserved car for smooth movement.

place.name: Hazrat Shah Jalal Mazar
place.description: Begin with the spiritual heart of Sylhet and a calm first stop.
place.tips: Keep the visit respectful and combine it with nearby food stops.

place.name: Lalakhal
place.description: A blue-green river stretch best enjoyed on a slow boat ride.
place.tips: Book a small boat in advance if it's a busy day.

place.name: Sylhet City Food Walk
place.description: Local snacks and a gentle evening around the city center.
why_visit: Low effort end to the day
place.tips: Try local tea and keep the pace slow after a long travel day.

days.title: Water & Tea Country
days.subtitle: Ratargul, Jaflong, and a soft countryside finish
days.transport: Leave early for wetland and border-river views.

place.name: Ratargul Swamp Forest
place.description: A floating green forest of water, roots, and boat paths.
place.tips: Carry a dry bag and keep your camera protected from splashes.

place.name: Jaflong
place.description: A wide open river-and-hills panorama with lots of room to slow down.
place.tips: Stay until the light softens for the best photos.

place.name: Madhabpur Lake
place.description: A quiet lake stop with enough calm to end the day slowly.
place.tips: Keep this stop for the last light and avoid rushing it.

packages.label: 🗓 3–4 Days
packages.summary: The best balanced Sylhet trip with city, water, tea gardens, and a trekking option.
packages.focus: For travelers who want Sylhet's full scenic variety.

days.title: Day 1 – Arrival & Tea Gardens
days.subtitle: Sylhet city introduction and a soft tea-country start
days.transport: Use a reserved car from the city center for an easy first day.

place.name: Hazrat Shah Jalal Mazar
place.description: A peaceful city start with history and local context.
place.tips: Visit respectfully and keep the first hour unhurried.

place.name: Srimangal Tea Gardens
place.description: Rows of tea plants and soft green hills make the countryside feel easy.
place.tips: Pair it with tea tasting or a local lunch.

place.name: Madhabpur Lake
place.description: A still lake with an easy walking loop and soft sunset light.
place.tips: Stay for golden hour if the weather is clear.

days.title: Day 2 – Swamp Forest & Water
days.subtitle: Ratargul and river landscapes at a slower pace
days.transport: Leave very early to keep the wetland timing comfortable.

place.name: Ratargul Swamp Forest
place.description: Boat through the flooded forest and hear the wetlands instead of traffic.
place.tips: Bring a dry bag, insect spray, and extra patience for the boat ride.

place.name: Lalakhal
place.description: A blue-green river stretch perfect for a relaxed boat cruise.
place.tips: Reserve a small boat if you are visiting on a holiday.

place.name: Bichanakandi
place.description: Stone fields and shallow water create one of Sylhet's most photogenic spots.
why_visit: Easy to pair with river stops
place.tips: Wear shoes with grip because the stones can be slippery.

days.title: Day 3 – Border Views & Scenic Finish
days.subtitle: Jaflong, Hum Hum option, and a slower evening return
days.transport: A hired car or jeep works best for this longer scenic day.

place.name: Jaflong
place.description: Open river edges, soft hills, and a classic Sylhet vista.
place.tips: Try to arrive before the crowds build.

place.name: Hum Hum Waterfall
place.description: A trekking-heavy waterfall option for travelers who want a little adventure.
why_visit: Different from the usual city route
place.tips: Use a guide and start early; this is the most demanding stop in the region.

place.name: Sylhet City Food Walk
place.description: Return to the city for dinner, tea, and a gentle wind-down.
place.tips: Use this as your flex evening if the waterfall leg takes longer.

days.title: Day 4 – Quiet Lake Loop
days.subtitle: A calm final day with tea-country air and slow exits
days.transport: Keep the day light if you have an evening bus or flight.

place.name: Madhabpur Lake
place.description: A simple and peaceful final stop in the tea belt.
place.tips: This works well as the final stop before heading back.

place.name: Bichanakandi
place.description: One more water-and-stone panorama before you leave Sylhet.
why_visit: Easy to fit into a return day
place.tips: Keep transport arranged in advance so you can leave on time.

place.name: Hazrat Shah Jalal Mazar
place.description: Return to the city for a short final visit before departure.
place.tips: Use the remaining time for tea and packing, not extra sightseeing.

packages.label: 🗓 7 Days
packages.summary: A full Sylhet circuit that stretches from tea gardens to waterfalls and slower heritage stops.
packages.focus: For travelers who want the region without rushing.

days.title: Day 1 – City Arrival
days.subtitle: City orientation and an easy start
days.transport: Use a reserved car for a comfortable arrival day.

place.name: Hazrat Shah Jalal Mazar
place.description: A calm opening stop and a cultural anchor point.
place.tips: Start slow and keep the day light after traveling in.

place.name: Sylhet City Food Walk
place.description: Try a few easy local dishes and settle into the pace.
place.tips: Do not overbook day one; leave room for rest.

place.name: Madhabpur Lake
place.description: Close the day with a peaceful lake view.
place.tips: Use this as a soft intro before longer day trips.

days.title: Day 2 – Tea Country
days.subtitle: Tea gardens, gentle hills, and tea tasting
days.transport: Drive toward Srimangal and keep a slow countryside pace.

place.name: Srimangal Tea Gardens
place.description: Long tea rows and soft hill contours define the region.
place.tips: Leave time for tea tasting or a local breakfast.

place.name: Lalakhal
place.description: A scenic boat stretch with clear blue-green water.
place.tips: Bring sun protection even on cooler days.

place.name: Madhabpur Lake
place.description: A sunset finish that keeps the day from feeling rushed.
place.tips: If weather turns, use a tea-house stop instead.

days.title: Day 3 – Wetlands
days.subtitle: Ratargul and nearby water routes
days.transport: Use an early driver and keep the day boat-focused.

place.name: Ratargul Swamp Forest
place.description: The most distinctive wetland landscape in the region.
place.tips: Keep valuables dry and leave enough time for the return drive.

place.name: Bichanakandi
place.description: A shallow water-and-stone platform that feels like a natural terrace.
place.tips: Avoid rushing the walking section if the stones are wet.

place.name: Sylhet Riverfront
place.description: A simple city wrap-up after a boat-heavy day.
place.tips: Keep dinner plans flexible because the boat segment can run late.

days.title: Day 4 – Border Views
days.subtitle: Jaflong, open river, and sunset reflections
days.transport: Reserve a jeep or car for the longer scenic route.

place.name: Jaflong
place.description: Open river views with hills in the distance.
place.tips: Move around the river edge slowly and stop for lunch nearby.

place.name: Lalakhal
place.description: A second river stop to keep the day relaxed rather than packed.
place.tips: This works especially well if you want an unhurried day.

place.name: Madhabpur Lake
place.description: Let the day end with a calm and easy lake view.
place.tips: If the weather is cloudy, use the stop simply as a rest break.

days.title: Day 5 – Trek Option
days.subtitle: Hum Hum Waterfall for active travelers
days.transport: Only do the trek if the weather and fitness level make sense.

place.name: Hum Hum Waterfall
place.description: A real hiking day with forest trail energy and a waterfall finish.
place.tips: Carry water, walk with a guide, and respect the trek time.

place.name: Tea Estate Rest Stop
place.description: An easy tea stop after the trek to recover slowly.
place.tips: Keep the rest of the day light after trekking.

place.name: Sylhet City Dinner
place.description: A good meal helps the day settle down after a trek.
place.tips: Book an early dinner because this day is physically heavier.

days.title: Day 6 – Quiet Countryside
days.subtitle: Tea gardens and an easy low-stress day
days.transport: Keep the pace flexible and avoid over-scheduling.

place.name: Srimangal Tea Gardens
place.description: Go back to tea country for a slow and scenic countryside day.
place.tips: This is a good day to breathe and not chase too many points.

place.name: Madhabpur Lake
place.description: A slow lake loop that keeps the mood relaxed.
place.tips: Pack tea and snacks if you're planning a picnic-style break.

place.name: Lalakhal
place.description: End with a river return when the light is softer.
place.tips: This day is intentionally slower than the others.

days.title: Day 7 – Departure Buffer
days.subtitle: Last breakfast, final views, and an easy exit
days.transport: Keep one simple last stop and then leave with buffer time.

place.name: Hazrat Shah Jalal Mazar
place.description: A gentle final city visit before leaving Sylhet.
place.tips: Do this only if your departure time leaves enough room.

place.name: Sylhet City Food Walk
place.description: One last food stop before you head home.
place.tips: Avoid a heavy lunch if you still have a long road journey.

place.name: Departure
place.description: Leave with enough margin so the trip ends smoothly.
place.tips: Pack earlier than usual; this day should feel calm rather than packed.

name: Cox's Bazar
tagline: Long beaches, sea air, and an easy holiday rhythm.
shortDescription: Cox's Bazar is Bangladesh's signature beach destination, blending long shoreline walks with cliff views, food stops, and the option to extend toward Saint Martin's Island.

bestSeason.label: November – February
bestSeason.note: Clear skies, calmer sea days, and the most comfortable beach weather.

whyVisit: Walk along the world's longest natural sea beach.
Blend beaches, cliffs, and local seafood in one easy trip.
Great for families, couples, and friends who want a coastal break.
A natural fit for day trips and longer sea-focused itineraries.
transportNotes: Use a local auto-rickshaw or reserved car for beach-to-beach movement.
Start early to avoid heat and to keep beach walks comfortable.
Keep a flexible end-of-day plan because sunsets can change the pace.

keyPlaces.name: Cox's Bazar Beach
keyPlaces.description: The main shoreline where long walks and simple sea views do the heavy lifting.
keyPlaces.tips: The beach is best when you keep the plan intentionally simple.

keyPlaces.name: Laboni Point
keyPlaces.description: A busy central stretch for people-watching, snacks, and quick beach access.
keyPlaces.tips: Use it as a practical beach hub rather than a long stop.

keyPlaces.name: Himchhari
keyPlaces.description: A hill-and-sea stop with views that break up the long beach stretch.
keyPlaces.tips: Keep the climb light if you're traveling with kids or seniors.

keyPlaces.name: Inani Beach
keyPlaces.description: A quieter beach segment known for stones, calm water, and a softer pace.
keyPlaces.tips: Bring water and keep an eye on the tide line.

keyPlaces.name: Ramu Buddhist Temple
keyPlaces.description: A cultural stop that adds heritage and calm to a sea-heavy itinerary.
keyPlaces.tips: Dress respectfully and combine it with a return-drive stop.

keyPlaces.name: Saint Martin's Island
keyPlaces.description: A coral-island day trip option for travelers who want a longer sea extension.
keyPlaces.tips: Mention it as a day-trip option and confirm sea conditions before booking.

packages.label: 🗓 1–2 Days
packages.summary: A beach-first Cox's Bazar break with easy shoreline time and one scenic side trip.
packages.focus: Perfect for a short sea holiday.

days.title: Arrival & Beach Walk
days.subtitle: Laboni Point and sunset on the main beach
days.transport: Keep the first day close to the beach belt.

place.name: Cox's Bazar Beach
place.description: Start with a long shoreline walk to set the tone.
place.tips: Do the longest walk before the heat rises.

place.name: Laboni Point
place.description: A practical center point for lunch, snacks, and beach access.
place.tips: Do not spend too long in the busiest zone if you want a calmer trip.

place.name: Beach Sunset
place.description: The simple joy of a sea sunset is enough for day one.
place.tips: Stay close to your hotel after dark if you have an early day tomorrow.

days.title: Cliff & Culture
days.subtitle: Himchhari and Ramu for a more rounded coastal day
days.transport: A reserved car keeps the second day efficient and comfortable.

place.name: Himchhari
place.description: Sea and hill views make this a neat change of scenery.
place.tips: Keep the climb light if you are with children or older travelers.

place.name: Ramu Buddhist Temple
place.description: A calm culture stop that balances the beach-heavy plan.
place.tips: Dress respectfully and keep the visit brief and calm.

place.name: Inani Beach
place.description: A quieter shore for a gentler finish to the trip.
place.tips: Check the tide line before going too close to the water.

packages.label: 🗓 3–4 Days
packages.summary: A well-balanced coastal stay with beach time, cliff views, and a Saint Martin's option.
packages.focus: Best for travelers who want more than a simple weekend at the shore.

days.title: Day 1 – Beach Orientation
days.subtitle: Laboni, long beach walks, and easy hotel check-in
days.transport: Stay near the central beach belt for the first night.

place.name: Cox's Bazar Beach
place.description: Settle in with a long beach walk and sea air.
place.tips: Keep the first day light so the trip feels restorative.

place.name: Laboni Point
place.description: Grab lunch and handle the practical beach logistics here.
place.tips: Use it as a launch point rather than the whole day.

place.name: Sunset Beach
place.description: The first sunset is often the most memorable coastal moment.
place.tips: Plan dinner after the sunset so you do not rush back and forth.

days.title: Day 2 – Himchhari & Inani
days.subtitle: Cliffs, quieter beaches, and a softer road loop
days.transport: A car or jeep is the easiest way to keep the day efficient.

place.name: Himchhari
place.description: A quick hill-and-sea stop that breaks the beach monotony.
place.tips: Keep the pace easy and do not overpack the day.

place.name: Inani Beach
place.description: A quieter shore where the trip slows down again.
place.tips: Use this beach if you prefer less crowding than the central shore.

place.name: Beachside Dinner
place.description: Local seafood and a slow end to the road loop.
place.tips: Book dinner early if you are traveling during peak season.

days.title: Day 3 – Culture & Optional Island Plan
days.subtitle: Ramu plus Saint Martin's as an optional extension
days.transport: Keep the day flexible because the island option depends on sea conditions.

place.name: Ramu Buddhist Temple
place.description: A calm heritage stop before another beach session.
place.tips: Keep footwear easy to remove and re-enter respectfully.

place.name: Saint Martin's Island Option
place.description: A day-trip extension for travelers who want a longer sea experience.
place.tips: Treat this as an optional add-on and confirm sea conditions first.

place.name: Laboni Point
place.description: Come back to the town center for an easy final walk.
place.tips: Do not over-schedule this day if you choose the island option.

days.title: Day 4 – Departure Buffer
days.subtitle: One last beach walk and a smooth exit
days.transport: Keep the last day intentionally light.

place.name: Cox's Bazar Beach
place.description: A final shoreline walk before packing up.
place.tips: Use the earliest calm period available.

place.name: Beach Lunch
place.description: Wrap the trip with a simple meal near the sea.
place.tips: Leave room to reach your bus, car, or flight comfortably.

place.name: Departure
place.description: Head out with a buffer rather than rushing the final hour.
place.tips: Pack earlier than usual so the day ends calmly.

packages.label: 🗓 7 Days
packages.summary: A relaxed week that mixes beach, culture, and a possible island extension.
packages.focus: For travelers who want the coast without rushing.

days.title: Day 1 – Arrival
days.subtitle: Beach orientation and sunset ease
days.transport: Stay close to the main beach zone to save time.

place.name: Cox's Bazar Beach
place.description: A long walk sets the week-long coastal pace.
place.tips: Take the walk before the heat builds.

place.name: Laboni Point
place.description: Handle lunch and practical beach needs here.
place.tips: Keep the first day simple and light.

place.name: Sunset Beach
place.description: A classic easy sunset closes the first day well.
place.tips: Dinner after sunset keeps the rhythm smooth.

days.title: Day 2 – Hill and Shore
days.subtitle: Himchhari and Inani for scenic contrast
days.transport: A car makes the longer road day easy.

place.name: Himchhari
place.description: Cliff views and sea horizon in a single stop.
place.tips: Keep the pace easy and avoid extra climbing if tired.

place.name: Inani Beach
place.description: A quieter shore for a slower block of the day.
place.tips: Bring water and avoid very high tide moments.

place.name: Beachside Dinner
place.description: Seafood and a quiet end to the road loop.
place.tips: Book ahead if you are here in the busiest season.

days.title: Day 3 – Heritage Day
days.subtitle: Ramu and a calmer inland reset
days.transport: Use a reserved car and keep the pace easy.

place.name: Ramu Buddhist Temple
place.description: A quiet heritage stop that breaks up the beach rhythm.
place.tips: Keep the visit respectful and compact.

place.name: Laboni Point
place.description: Return to the main zone for easy access and lunch.
place.tips: Use this to rest rather than to rush around.

place.name: Beach Sunset
place.description: Another slow sunset to keep the trip balanced.
place.tips: Let the day breathe instead of packing in more stops.

days.title: Day 4 – Optional Island Day
days.subtitle: Saint Martin's if sea conditions allow
days.transport: Only proceed if the weather and ferry schedule are comfortable.

place.name: Saint Martin's Island
place.description: A full-day island extension for travelers who want extra sea time.
place.tips: Treat it as an optional trip and check sea conditions before booking.

place.name: Island Beach Time
place.description: Slow beach time is the point, not an overstuffed agenda.
place.tips: Carry cash and keep your belongings minimal.

place.name: Return to Cox's Bazar
place.description: Head back with enough buffer to avoid a rushed close.
place.tips: If the ferry plan changes, keep the beach day as your fallback.

name: Sundarbans
tagline: Mangrove silence, wildlife, and tidal forest drama.
shortDescription: The Sundarbans is a nature-forward escape of boat rides, mangroves, and wildlife watching. It suits travelers who want a more adventurous, wildlife-centered Bangladesh trip.

bestSeason.label: November – February
bestSeason.note: Dry season travel gives the best boat comfort and wildlife chances.

whyVisit: Wildlife and river navigation in one journey.
transportNotes: Use organized boat tours and stay on a clear schedule.
Carry light luggage and expect a full boat-focused day.

keyPlaces.name: Mangrove Boat Route
keyPlaces.description: The core boat route where the forest feels close and tidal.
keyPlaces.tips: Book a guide with a clear safety plan.

keyPlaces.name: Forest Watch Point
keyPlaces.description: Quiet lookout stops are where the mangrove atmosphere lands best.
keyPlaces.tips: Use binoculars if you have them.

keyPlaces.name: River Mouth View
keyPlaces.description: Where river flow and tidal water create a unique finish.
keyPlaces.tips: Morning light is the most forgiving.

keyPlaces.name: Village Stay
keyPlaces.description: A simple cultural stop that adds local rhythm to the eco-trip.
keyPlaces.tips: Keep respectful distance when photographing people.

packages.label: 🗓 1–2 Days
packages.summary: A simple eco-break with boat rides and one wildlife outlook.
packages.focus: Good for first-time nature travelers.

packages.label: 🗓 3–4 Days
packages.summary: More time on the water with a better chance to slow down.
packages.focus: Best balance of boat time and rest.

packages.label: 🗓 7 Days
packages.summary: An extended wildlife trip with more river navigation and village time.
packages.focus: For serious eco travelers.

name: Dhaka
tagline: Heritage lanes, city food, and the energy of the capital.
shortDescription: Dhaka mixes history, food, and city life in a way that works well for short, culture-rich itineraries. It's the easiest starting point for international arrivals and city-loving locals.

bestSeason.label: November – February
bestSeason.note: Best for walking heritage lanes and trying food without heavy heat.

transportNotes: Use rideshares and short walking segments.
Keep traffic time in mind when planning the day.

keyPlaces.name: Lalbagh Fort
keyPlaces.description: The classic Mughal-era stop for heritage lovers.
keyPlaces.tips: Go early to avoid the worst heat and traffic.

keyPlaces.name: Ahsan Manzil
keyPlaces.description: A heritage palace that pairs well with Old Dhaka food walks.
keyPlaces.tips: Combine it with a longer Old Dhaka route.

keyPlaces.name: Old Dhaka Food Lane
keyPlaces.description: The best way to experience the city's living food culture.
keyPlaces.tips: Try a mixed snack walk instead of a full heavy meal.

keyPlaces.name: Sadarghat
keyPlaces.description: The river port area adds movement and raw city texture.
keyPlaces.tips: Stay alert and keep camera gear secure.

packages.label: 🗓 1–2 Days
packages.summary: City highlights, heritage, and a compact food trail.
packages.focus: Good for a quick urban break.

packages.label: 🗓 3–4 Days
packages.summary: A better mix of heritage, food, and slower city movement.
packages.focus: Best for first-time city visitors.

packages.label: 🗓 7 Days
packages.summary: A dense city week built around food, history, and neighborhoods.
packages.focus: For travelers who like urban exploration.

name: Chittagong
tagline: Harbor energy, hills, and a taste of coastal city life.
shortDescription: Chittagong sits between sea, hills, and commerce, giving it a very different rhythm from Dhaka. It's great for travelers who want food, city energy, and easy access toward the coast.

bestSeason.label: November – February
bestSeason.note: Comfortable for city movement and hill-view stops.

transportNotes: Use a car for hill stops and city hops.
Traffic can be heavy, so keep your plan compact.

keyPlaces.name: Patenga Beach
keyPlaces.description: An easy coastal stop on the city edge.
keyPlaces.tips: Use it as a wind-down stop rather than a full beach day.

keyPlaces.name: Foy's Lake
keyPlaces.description: A pleasant lake-and-hill attraction for city visitors.
keyPlaces.tips: Pair it with an early dinner in the city.

keyPlaces.name: Chittagong Hill View
keyPlaces.description: A quick reminder that the city sits close to the hills.
keyPlaces.tips: Do not make this a long stop on a traffic-heavy day.

keyPlaces.name: Baitul Falah area
keyPlaces.description: An urban food and mosque area that works well for short local exploration.
keyPlaces.tips: Use a short ride rather than attempting a long walk in peak traffic.

packages.label: 🗓 1–2 Days
packages.summary: A city-coast combo with enough room for a beach sunset.
packages.focus: Good for a quick coastal city break.

packages.label: 🗓 3–4 Days
packages.summary: A broader Chittagong stay with city food and a seaside finish.
packages.focus: Best for city and sea mix.

packages.label: 🗓 7 Days
packages.summary: A slower harbor week with more room for nearby day trips.
packages.focus: For travelers who like city rhythm.

name: Bandarban
tagline: Hills, viewpoints, and trekking energy in one escape.
shortDescription: Bandarban is the go-to destination for travelers who want hills, viewpoints, and more active outdoor days. It is especially strong for couples, friends, and nature-focused trips.

bestSeason.label: October – March
bestSeason.note: Dry, clear weather is best for viewpoints and trekking.

transportNotes: Use a jee/vehicle and keep the road time in mind.
Trekking days need an early start and enough rest.

keyPlaces.name: Nilgiri Viewpoint
keyPlaces.description: One of the most accessible hill viewpoints in the district.
keyPlaces.tips: Go early if you want a clear horizon.

keyPlaces.name: Nafakhum area
keyPlaces.description: A trekking-heavy area for travelers who want a bigger outdoor day.
keyPlaces.tips: Use a guide and do not rush the trail.

keyPlaces.name: Bandarban Town View
keyPlaces.description: A practical stop for food and re-grouping between hill drives.
keyPlaces.tips: Use it as the resting base for hill trips.

keyPlaces.name: Nilachal
keyPlaces.description: A scenic viewpoint that works well on a soft hill itinerary.
keyPlaces.tips: Combine it with dinner back in town.

packages.label: 🗓 1–2 Days
packages.summary: A hill-view sampler with one viewpoint and one easy city stop.
packages.focus: Good for first hill trips.

packages.label: 🗓 3–4 Days
packages.summary: The right amount of time for viewpoints and one trek day.
packages.focus: Best overall Bandarban balance.

packages.label: 🗓 7 Days
packages.summary: A longer hill circuit for travelers who want more trekking and slow stays.
packages.focus: For outdoor-focused trips.

name: Rajshahi
tagline: Riverside calm, heritage, and a slower northwestern rhythm.
shortDescription: Rajshahi is a smooth cultural and riverside destination with a strong sense of calm. It suits travelers who like history, short city walks, and a slower pace.

bestSeason.label: November – February
bestSeason.note: Comfortable weather for walking and riverfront time.

transportNotes: Use city rides and short walks by the river.
Keep the heat in mind if you travel outside winter.

keyPlaces.name: Padma Riverfront
keyPlaces.description: A calm river edge that gives Rajshahi its relaxed tone.
keyPlaces.tips: Keep a light pace and use it as a slow-down stop.

keyPlaces.name: Varendra Research Museum
keyPlaces.description: A heritage stop for travelers who like local history.
keyPlaces.tips: Combine it with nearby lunch or tea.

keyPlaces.name: Puthia Temple Complex
keyPlaces.description: A heritage cluster that expands the Rajshahi day beyond the city center.
keyPlaces.tips: Carry water and keep the day balanced with a slower lunch.

keyPlaces.name: Local Mango Market
keyPlaces.description: A seasonal stop that gives the city a very local flavor.
keyPlaces.tips: Best when mango season is in full swing.

packages.label: 🗓 1–2 Days
packages.summary: A short cultural break with riverfront calm and one heritage stop.
packages.focus: Good for a quiet city reset.

packages.label: 🗓 3–4 Days
packages.summary: A better-paced Rajshahi trip with river, temple, and museum time.
packages.focus: Best for a slow cultural stay.

packages.label: 🗓 7 Days
packages.summary: A full northwestern city-and-heritage loop with a slower pace.
packages.focus: For relaxed travelers.

name: Sreemangal
tagline: Tea estates, quiet lakes, and countryside calm.
shortDescription: Sreemangal is the tea capital of Bangladesh and a natural fit for slow, scenic trips. It is especially good for couples, families, and tea-loving travelers.

bestSeason.label: October – March
bestSeason.note: Comfortable for countryside stays and tea estate visits.

transportNotes: Use a car or reserved auto for tea-estate movement.
Keep the itinerary slow and scenic.

keyPlaces.name: Tea Estates
keyPlaces.description: The main reason travelers come here: green tea rows and quiet air.
keyPlaces.tips: Add tea tasting if it is offered by the estate.

keyPlaces.name: Lawachara edge
keyPlaces.description: A forest-edge stop for travelers who want a bit more nature.
keyPlaces.tips: Keep the walk short if you are traveling with kids.

keyPlaces.name: Madhabpur Lake
keyPlaces.description: A calm lake view that fits the Sreemangal mood perfectly.
keyPlaces.tips: Use this as the final scenic stop of the day.

keyPlaces.name: Baa Khol
keyPlaces.description: A quieter countryside stop for fresh air and a slower walk.
keyPlaces.tips: Keep it simple and do not overpack the day.

packages.label: 🗓 1–2 Days
packages.summary: Tea gardens, one forest-edge stop, and a lake finish.
packages.focus: Great for a quick countryside break.

packages.label: 🗓 3–4 Days
packages.summary: A slower tea-country trip with room for walking and rest.
packages.focus: Best for peace and tea lovers.

packages.label: 🗓 7 Days
packages.summary: A long tea-country stay with extra countryside time.
packages.focus: For travelers who want to slow all the way down.
